channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Sin duda, el peor presidente de la historia de EE. UU.’
item.description: El daño que el presidente Donald Trump le ha infligido a Estados Unidos, y al mundo, es tan enorme y amplio que resulta difícil de comprender.
item.dc:creator: Thomas B. Edsall

item.title: Pesca urbana en los canales de la ‘Pequeña Venecia’
item.description: Los canales de Treviso, Italia, están repletos de carpas, cachos y truchas, que invitan a los pescadores a vadear y descubrir un mundo sereno oculto en el corazón de una ciudad.
item.dc:creator: Paul Abercrombie and Matteo de Mayda

media:content.media:description: Para Damiano Molon, un guía de Treviso, Italia, compartir los pozos de pesca de su ciudad natal con los visitantes le da la oportunidad de hacer lo que le gusta.

item.title: Bruce, el loro sin pico que se convirtió en macho alfa
item.description: La kea se hizo famosa por aprender a usar guijarros para limpiarse. Su siguiente innovación dejó atónitos a los científicos.
item.dc:creator: Carl Zimmer

item.title: El sarampión se llevó a mi hija. Quiero que todos sepan esto
item.description: Si más gente se hubiera vacunado, ella aún podría estar aquí.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Archer

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración fotográfica de Amy Friend para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ilustración fotográfica basada en una fotografía de Renae Walker facilitada por la autora, su madre, Rebecca Archer.

item.title: Advirtió sobre los peligros de la IA. Ojalá su padre hubiera hecho caso
item.description: Ben Riley, quien escribe un boletín sobre los riesgos de los chatbots, no supo qué hacer cuando su padre empezó a confiar en la inteligencia artificial para manejar su diagnóstico de cáncer.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Rosenbluth

media:content.media:credit: Jordan Vonderhaar para The New York Times

item.title: Un tesoro musical: 10.000 conciertos grabados en Chicago ahora en línea
item.description: Aadam Jacobs grabó miles de shows desde la década de 1980, entre ellos de Nirvana antes de la fama. Ahora unos voluntarios digitalizan su colección para convertirla en un archivo histórico de libre acceso.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

media:content.media:description: Aadam Jacobs en su casa de Chicago. El Chicago Tribune lo describió, entre otras cosas, como “un incansable documentalista de audio” por grabar miles de conciertos íntimos en su ciudad natal.

item.title: Así se viste al ‘diablo’ a la moda
item.description: La gira de prensa de El diablo viste a la moda 2 ha sido un desfile de moda para sus estrellas y sus estilistas, quienes han confiado en los chats grupales, los pantalones deportivos y el trabajo en equipo para lograrlo.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: ¿’Donnyland’? Ucrania propone rebautizar parte del Donbás en honor a Trump
item.description: La propuesta refleja una realidad global en la que los gobiernos apelan a la vanidad del presidente Trump para poner el poderío estadounidense de su parte.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Andrew E. Kramer

media:content.media:credit: Lynsey Addario for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sloviansk, in the industrial Donbas region, which was first invaded by Russia in 2014.

item.title: Picture Prompt | 36 Hours in Your Hometown
item.description: If you were writing a travel guide to the place where you live, what would you recommend people do?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Business as Usual at T Magazine, Aside From the Dancing on the Desks
item.description: “Live From the 10th Floor,” a video series, invites actors, musicians and other artists to perform at The Times’s Midtown Manhattan headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Coleman

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 22, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Michael Kosta Introduces Former Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer
item.description: “It’s always the ones you never heard of,” the late night host said on Tuesday’s “The Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Virginia Voters Approve New Map Favoring Democrats
item.description: Virginia voters approved a new map that could flip four House seats away from Republicans going into the 2026 midterm elections. It was the latest fight in the national redistricting war.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

item.description: People carried on with grocery shopping as a strong earthquake shook a store in Hachinohe, a city in northern Japan, on Monday.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘propensity’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 77 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: propensity
item.description: This word has appeared in 77 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Dave Mason, Songwriter Who Co-Founded Traffic, Dies at 79
item.description: The English singer and guitarist wrote mainstays of the classic-rock canon like “Feelin’ Alright?” and “Only You Know and I Know.” He also had a successful solo career.
item.dc:creator: Jim Farber

media:content.media:description: Dave Mason performing in Chicago in 1980.

item.title: How China Is Building Its Next Outpost at Sea
item.description: China has been quietly and quickly building an island in disputed waters off of the coast of Vietnam over the last few months.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo and Agnes Chang

item.title: Pope Leo Is Skipping Some of Africa’s Biggest Catholic Nations
item.description: As the pontiff arrives in the tiny authoritarian nation of Equatorial Guinea, some Catholics in Africa say they are excited about his visit but are feeling a little left out.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in Cameroon on Friday.

item.title: Quiz: Can You Tell Real British Insults From Fakes?
item.description: Researchers in the U.K. are working to document the country’s array of obscure invective.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Testani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.

item.title: A Year After Terrorist Attack, a Kashmir Town Longs for Tourists
item.description: Pahalgam, with steep ravines, grassy hillsides and pine forests, is a base for Hindu pilgrimages that business operators hope will revive visitors.
item.dc:creator: Showkat Nanda

media:content.media:credit: Showkat Nanda for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Security officers are a constant presence on tourist routes in Pahalgam after last year’s attack.

item.title: Corrections: April 22, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, April 22, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Tender Are the Notes: 8 Keats Letters Reappear
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, April 22, 2026.

item.title: U.S. Sends Plane to Cuba to Get Child in Transgender Custody Case
item.description: The F.B.I. believed the child was kidnapped by a transgender parent to potentially undergo gender transition surgery, according to court filings.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: A flight tracker showed that a U.S. government plane landed at the Jose Marti International Airport in Havana. It appeared to be part of an F.B.I. mission to retrieve an American child.

item.title: Two Drugs Stir Hope for Treatment of Deadly Pancreatic Cancer
item.description: In separate small clinical trials, two treatments showed promise that they could help patients with one of the most dire diagnoses in oncology.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins and Gina Kolata

media:content.media:description: An animated image showing the human body highlighting the pancreas.

item.title: Olafur Eliasson Uses Art and Sound to Raise Climate Awareness in Utah
item.description: The Danish Icelandic artist reflects on his childhood and how it influenced and empowered his art.
item.dc:creator: Farah Nayeri

item.title: Man Is Charged With Providing Gun to Louisiana Shooter
item.description: The arrest came two days after a gunman carried out a rampage that left eight children dead in Shreveport and injured two adults.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales

media:content.media:description: A vigil mourned eight children fatally shot in Shreveport, La.

item.title: The Vienna Climate Biennale Contrasts Chaos with Hope
item.description: In a world marked by ecological, social and political crises, the art festival aims to get people talking, thinking and ready to imagine a better world.
item.dc:creator: Keridwen Cornelius

item.title: A Mural by the Painter of ‘American Gothic’ Gets New Life
item.description: Grant Wood’s 1926 “Corn Room” has been restored and will anchor an exhibition celebrating its centennial at the Sioux City Art Center in Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Alex V. Cipolle

media:content.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: From the left, Rita Berg, Tailor Choquette, Alexa Beller, and Kristy Jeffcoat working on Grant Wood’s “Corn Room” mural at the Midwest Art Conservation Center in Minneapolis.

item.title: Virginia Passes Gerrymandered Map to Help Democrats in Midterms: 4 Takeaways
item.description: Virginia’s approval of an aggressive gerrymander could give Democrats up to four additional House seats as they seek to win back Congress.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:description: National party leaders were heavily invested in Virginia’s referendum, and tens of millions of dollars flowed in.

item.title: An Installation in Nature has Climate Lessons for Humans
item.description: With lichen, mechanical artwork and a barrel of snowflakes, “Climate Clock” will wind along a trail through the forests outside Oulu, Finland.
item.dc:creator: David Belcher

item.title: The Haas Brothers’ Creative Creations Are on Tour
item.description: A midcareer survey of the work by the twins Simon Haas and Nikolai Haas is touring the country.
item.dc:creator: Ted Loos

media:content.media:credit: Stella Kalinina for The New York Times

item.title: Immigration Agent Is Charged With Assaulting a Colorado Protester
item.description: The officer faces a misdemeanor charge after an encounter in Colorado, becoming one of the few federal agents to be prosecuted by local officials during the immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy

media:content.media:credit: Nina Riggio for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Franci Stagi, the protester the officer is seen grabbing on video last year in Colorado, was happy to see charges but felt they were too lenient.

item.title: Read the document
item.description: New York utilities present their findings concerning new statewide heat protection rules.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers: Wednesday, April 22, 2026
item.description: Hoang-Kim Vu brings us an awesome Wednesday puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Aronow

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Bright for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s What the New Virginia House Map Looks Like
item.description: Democrats now hold six of the state’s 11 seats in the U.S. House, but the new map could allow the party to win 10 of them.
item.dc:creator: Elena Shao

item.title: Here’s Where the National Fight Over Gerrymandered Maps Stands
item.description: With Virginia’s vote on Tuesday, Democrats pulled close to even with Republicans, who may have further cards to play in the race to gain extra House seats.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

item.title: Review: In ‘The Balusters,’ Neighborly Dysfunction Is on the Agenda
item.description: David Lindsay-Abaire’s comedy about a wealthy homeowners association thrown into disarray makes a case for the same social compact it skewers.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:description: Clockwise from top left, Ricardo Chavira, Carl Clemons-Hopkins, Richard Thomas, Marylouise Burke, Jeena Yi, Kayli Carter and Anika Noni Rose in “The Balusters,” at the Samuel J. Friedman in Manhattan.

item.title: Soldados israelíes condenados a prisión por dañar una estatua de Jesús en Líbano
item.description: Una estatua de la crucifixión de Cristo fue sustituida después de que un soldado fotografiara a otro que parecía estar golpeando la cabeza de la imagen con un mazo.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger

item.title: What Was the 2015 Iran Nuclear Deal?
item.description: President Trump has ridiculed President Barack Obama and the agreement, which he withdrew from during his first term. His critics say he could have avoided a war had he left it in place.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

media:content.media:description: Negotiators from the United States, Iran and European countries meeting to discuss a nuclear deal in Vienna in 2015.

item.title: Poetry Challenge Day 3: W.H. Auden, The Poet and His Technique
item.description: It helps to be a little heartsick — and the best poet of your time. Our weeklong poetry challenge continues, including W.H. Auden himself reading “The More Loving One.”
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott and Aliza Aufrichtig

item.title: With $116 Million Gift, National Gallery Will Send Its Art Around Nation
item.description: The large contribution from the billionaire collector Mitchell P. Rales is enabling long-term loans to smaller museums in perpetuity.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pogrebin

item.title: Virginia Passes New House Map in a Midterm Victory for Democrats
item.description: The map, as extreme a gerrymander as exists in the United States, could let Democrats win 10 of the state’s 11 House seats as the party tries to win back the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:description: A voter casting their ballot during early voting in Burke, Va.

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Upholds Texas Ten Commandments Law
item.description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit said the law does not violate the Constitution. The plaintiffs said they planned to ask the Supreme Court to reverse the decision.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Ten Commandments poster in an English classroom at Fossil Ridge High School in Fort Worth.

item.title: Anger Evident in Red Areas as Virginians Cast Their Votes
item.description: “It’s just unfair for the Democrats to have 10 seats and the Republicans to have one,” one voter said of the proposed House map. But others in the state’s blue northern areas were more supportive.
item.dc:creator: Nan K. Chase, Leah Small and Darren Sands

media:content.media:description: Members of the Madison County Republican Committee demonstrated in opposition of the Virginia redistricting referendum during early voting earlier this month in Madison, Va.

item.title: Devin Nunes Departs Trump Media After 4 Years as C.E.O.
item.description: The former congressman said it was an “appropriate time” for a leadership change at Truth Social’s parent company, whose share price has floundered.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Goldstein

media:content.media:description: Former Representative Devin Nunes with President Trump, who chose him to run Trump Media & Technology in late 2021.

item.title: The Hypocrisy of OpenAI and Palantir
item.description: A super PAC that’s funded by co-founders of Palantir, OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz is spending millions on attack ads opposing Alex Bores’s campaign for Congress. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the New York state assemblyman explains how tech companies have targeted his pro-regulation positions, despite publicly claiming to welcome A.I. oversight.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: A Real Plan for A.I. Job Losses
item.description: What if the A.I. boom takes not just your job, but most jobs? The congressional candidate Alex Bores joins the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein to pitch an “A.I. Dividend” — a policy devised to fund a universal basic income if A.I. begins to outmatch human labor.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: The New Virginia Map Could Add Four Democratic Seats
item.description: The party now holds six of the state’s 11 seats in the U.S. House, but the proposed map could allow Democrats to win 10 of them.
item.dc:creator: Elena Shao

item.title: Proposed Lifetime Smoking Ban to Become Law in Britain
item.description: The proposal, which was approved by Parliament on Tuesday, will ban the supply or sale of tobacco products to anyone born in 2009 or after, permanently.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: The proposed lifetime smoking ban will apply in England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland.
